title: [HISTORICAL] Core Refactoring (v0.6.0)
description:
## Summary
Core refactoring work for v0.6.0 release including nilearn integration, deprecation stubs, and method updates.

## Key Achievements
- Deleted deprecated Priority 3 files (brain_collection, model specs)
- Added deprecation stubs for removed methods (predict, ttest, randomise, predict_multi)
- Integrated nilearn for .regress() functionality (7 tests)
- Added .compute_contrasts() method (3 tests)
- Refactored .extract_roi() to use NiftiLabelsMasker
- Updated .smooth() and .empty() to return copies
- Maintained HDF5 backward compatibility

## Key Commits
- fb256c8 Fix compute_contrasts() to work with new .fit() API
- b514a8a Remove regress() method in v0.6.0 breaking release
- 6ce243b Optimize BrainData methods: replace deepcopy with shallow copy
- c2a0929 test: Add filter() and compute_contrasts() tests
- ce3662d fix: Complete Round 1 audit fixes - 4 critical bugs resolved

## Reference
- claude-research/refactor.md (original goals)
- claude-research/refactor-todos.md (Priority 1 section)

title: Investigate test_brain_data.py slowness
description:
## Summary
test_brain_data.py is the slowest test file. Investigate why and identify optimization opportunities.

## Analysis Needed
1. Profile test runtime breakdown (which tests are slowest?)
2. Are we using sim_brain_data (slow) where minimal_brain_data would work?
3. Are there redundant computations within tests?
4. Can we use more module-scoped fixtures?
5. Are we testing nilearn internals unnecessarily?

## Deliverables
- Runtime profile of test_brain_data.py
- List of optimization opportunities
- Implement high-impact improvements

title: Implement two_sample permutation test
description: GPU-accelerated two-sample permutation test. Tests: 17. Commit: 6492d51

title: Ridge CV: parallel parameter receives Backend object instead of string
description:
## Summary
solve_ridge_cv() receives Backend object instead of string for parallel parameter.

## Error
```
ValueError: parallel must be None, 'cpu', or 'gpu', got: <nltools.backends.Backend object at 0x...>
```

## Location
- nltools/models/ridge.py:197 calls solve_ridge_cv with `parallel=self.backend_`
- nltools/algorithms/ridge/solvers.py:766 validates parallel param

## Affected Tests
- test_ridge_auto_detects_single_space
- test_ridge_cv_fits_and_selects_alpha  
- test_ridge_cv_alphas_parameter
- test_ridge_cv_multi_target

## Fix
ridge.py should pass the backend name string, not the Backend object.

title: [HISTORICAL] Stats Migration to Inference (v0.6.0)
description:
## Summary
Complete migration of stats.py functions to the GPU-accelerated inference module.

## Key Achievements
- Phase 1: Code deduplication (_calc_pvalue → _compute_pvalue)
- Phase 2: Deprecation & removal (correlation, _permute_sign, regress, etc.)
- Phase 3: Matrix utilities to inference/matrix.py (double_center, u_center, distance_correlation)
- isc() wrapper migration with return key mapping fix
- isc_group() wrapper migration with return key mapping fix
- isfc() migration with _compute_cross_correlation()
- CPU parallelization (n_jobs=-1 default)
- Phase 5: Usage verification (threshold, multi_threshold, summarize_bootstrap)
- 9 statistical correctness tests

## Key Commits
- 4670656 finish refactoring stats and replace pandas with polars/numpy ops
- bd47967 improve isc group
- 1a2dd5f standardize inference
- 433e462 add isc and matrix correctness tests
- 3d1b7fc refactor inference tests and add two sample statistical correctness tests
- ebdd51a optimize icc, improve memory management, refactor inference module

title: Handle NaN values in Adjacency.similarity() (#432)

## Tasks

title: Cortical flatmap visualization
description:
## Goal
Explore adding cortical flatmap visualization capabilities to nltools, enabling users to quickly create flattened cortical surface visualizations.

## Inspiration/Resources
- **autoflatten**: https://github.com/gallantlab/autoflatten - Automatic cortical flatmap generation
- **pycortex**: https://gallantlab.org/pycortex/ - Comprehensive cortical surface visualization library
- **nilearn surface plotting**: Existing functionality we can build on

## Key Questions to Explore
1. What's the simplest path to flatmap viz? (pycortex integration vs custom implementation)
2. Can we leverage nilearn's existing surface utilities as a foundation?
3. What freesurfer/atlas dependencies are acceptable?
4. Should this be optional dependency to keep nltools lightweight?

## Potential Approaches
- Thin wrapper around pycortex for quick wins
- Integration with nilearn's surface plotting (surf_plotting module)
- Use autoflatten for generating flatmaps from freesurfer outputs

## Notes
Discussion item for v0.6.0 or future release - assess scope and complexity first.

title: Identify test coverage gaps and remove redundancy
description:
## Summary
Find genuine gaps in test coverage while identifying tests that can be safely removed.

## Analysis Needed

### Coverage Gaps
1. Run coverage report to find untested code paths
2. Identify critical functionality without tests
3. Focus on: error handling, edge cases, new features

### Redundancy Removal
1. Tests that duplicate other tests
2. Tests that verify external library behavior
3. Tests that are too granular (testing implementation details)
4. Tests for deprecated functionality

## Tools
- pytest-cov for coverage analysis
- Manual review of test/code ratio

## Deliverables
- Coverage report with gap analysis
- List of tests recommended for removal with justification
- New tests for critical gaps
- Net test count change estimate

description:

Major performance enhancements and architectural improvements for v0.7.0.

### Storage Migration

title: Reorganize tests: API contracts vs computations
description:
## Summary
Distinguish between tests that verify our API contracts vs tests that verify computational correctness of wrapped libraries.

## Philosophy
- **API contract tests**: Verify parameters, return types, shapes, error handling
  - Fast, use minimal fixtures
  - Test OUR code's behavior
  
- **Computation tests**: Verify mathematical correctness
  - May be slower, need realistic data
  - Should NOT re-test numpy/nilearn/sklearn - they have their own tests

## Analysis Needed
1. Categorize existing tests into API vs computation
2. Identify tests that just wrap numpy/nilearn calls and re-test them
3. Find tests that conflate API and computation (split them)

## Examples
```python
# BAD: Testing numpy's mean() through our wrapper
def test_mean_calculation():
    result = brain_data.mean()
    expected = np.mean(brain_data.data)
    assert result == expected  # We're just testing numpy!

# GOOD: Testing our API contract
def test_mean_returns_brain_data():
    result = brain_data.mean()
    assert isinstance(result, BrainData)
    assert result.shape == expected_shape
```

## Deliverables
- Test categorization document
- Recommendations for tests to remove/modify
- Implement changes

title: [HISTORICAL] Code Cleanup & Efficient Copying (v0.6.0)
description:
## Summary
Code cleanup work implementing efficient copying patterns and property decorators for cleaner API.

## Key Achievements
- Implemented _shallow_copy_with_data() helper (14 tests)
- Updated 10 methods with efficient copying pattern (~80% performance improvement)
- Converted .shape() to @property
- Converted .isempty() to @property
- Converted .dtype() to @property
- Updated ~90 property calls across codebase

## Key Commits
- 6ce243b Optimize BrainData methods: replace deepcopy with shallow copy
- 759d32f refactor(api): Convert .shape(), .isempty(), .dtype() to properties + quick fixes

## Reference
- claude-research/refactor-todos.md (Priority 1.5 section)

title: Test suite deep audit and reorganization
description:
## Summary
Comprehensive audit of the test suite to improve organization, reduce runtime, and ensure we're testing the right things.

## Context
Following wqz (streamline test infrastructure), we reduced redundant computations. Now we need a deeper look at:
1. What we're testing (API contracts vs implementation details)
2. How tests are organized (markers, tiers)
3. Where gaps exist vs unnecessary coverage

## Goals
- Faster test suite with same confidence
- Clear distinction: API contract tests vs computational correctness tests
- Remove tests that just re-test numpy/nilearn/sklearn internals
- Identify genuine gaps in coverage

title: Streamline test infrastructure
description:
## Summary
Streamline test infrastructure to reduce redundant computation, consolidate fixtures, and improve test runtime.

## Primary Goal: Reduce Redundant Computation
**Key principle: Multiple assertions per expensive operation.**

### Pattern to Eliminate
```python
# BAD: 5 fits when 1 would suffice
def test_orthogonality(self, data):
    srm = SRM().fit(data)  # 2s
    assert orthogonal(srm.w_)

def test_reconstruction(self, data):
    srm = SRM().fit(data)  # 2s again!
    assert reconstructs(srm)
```

### Target Pattern
```python
# GOOD: 1 fit, multiple assertions
@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def fitted_srm(multi_subject_data):
    return SRM().fit(multi_subject_data)

class TestSRMMathematicalProperties:
    def test_fitted_model_properties(self, fitted_srm, multi_subject_data):
        # All mathematical invariants in one test
        assert orthogonal(fitted_srm.w_)
        assert reconstructs(fitted_srm)
        assert correct_shape(fitted_srm.s_)
        
        # Transform once, assert multiple properties
        transformed = fitted_srm.transform(multi_subject_data)
        assert correct_transform_shape(transformed)
        assert variance_preserved(transformed)
```

## Phase 1: Audit Expensive Operations
- [ ] Identify all expensive ops: fit(), transform(), regress(), permutation tests
- [ ] Map test→computation redundancy (e.g., SRM: 30 fits for 34 tests)
- [ ] Prioritize by impact: SRM, HyperAlignment, inference tests

## Phase 2: Consolidate Assertions
- [ ] Group mathematical property tests → single test with multiple asserts
- [ ] Use module-scoped fixtures for fitted models
- [ ] Separate mutation tests that need fresh fixtures

## Phase 3: Fixture Optimization
- [ ] Merge overlapping fixtures (minimal_brain_data variants)
- [ ] Parametrize H5 path fixtures
- [ ] Move reusable fixtures to conftest.py
- [ ] Document scope choices (function vs module)

## Phase 4: Cleanup
- [ ] Remove duplicate fixtures (e.g., two fitted_hyperalignment in same file)
- [ ] Add skip markers with reasons for platform-specific tests
- [ ] Ensure all tests have tier markers

## Success Metrics
- Reduce fit() calls by 50%+ in core/ tests
- Maintain test coverage and isolation where needed
- Reduce tier1 runtime from ~18s

## Tradeoffs
- Grouped assertions = harder to pinpoint failures (mitigate with clear assert messages)
- Module scope = tests must not mutate fixtures (document clearly)
close_reason: Completed Phase 1-3 consolidation. Reduced fit() calls: SRM 30→20 (with module fixtures), HyperAlignment 19→12, Models 44→41. Added module-scoped fixtures, consolidated property tests. Discovered pre-existing Ridge CV bug (filed as 37v).
